boards.title: Grand Challenge
boards.description: Mixed review: draws problems across several topics in one station (boss level).

views.description: Adding two decimals, lined up on the decimal point.
Adding fractions with a like denominator: a/n + b/n.
An angle sweeping open to a given number of degrees.
A rectangle filling with unit squares to teach area.
A rectangular array of dots — multiplication as rows × columns.
Base-ten blocks: tens-rods and ones-cubes for two-digit place value.
A single big numeral or label, for emphasis / answer-reveal beats.
An analog clock set to a time, for telling time.
A tray of coins to count a money amount / make change.
Vertical column arithmetic (add/sub/mul). The author sets each beat's state to walk a worked example column by column.

fields.name: result
fields.note: digits; use spaces for not-yet-filled places

views.description: Two groups of blocks sliding together to show addition as combining.
Two groups of objects with a < > = "hungry mouth" sign that eats the bigger amount.
Two number cards side by side to compare magnitudes.
Two decimals aligned by place value for comparing.
Two fractions side by side (k1/n1 vs k2/n2) for comparing.
A hundred-grid shading a decimal value (tenths/hundredths).
Multiplying/dividing a decimal by 10/100 — the point hops places.
Two clocks (start and end) showing elapsed time.
Equivalent fractions: a/b scaled by m to show why they are equal.
Dots paired up to show whether a number is even (all paired) or odd (one left over).
A fraction bar split into n parts with k shaded.
A fraction as a pie and a bar: k of n parts shaded.
A total shared into equal groups — division as sharing.
A long-division bracket walked step by step (Divide–Multiply–Subtract–Bring down).

fields.name: quoSteps
fields.note: step at which each quotient digit appears

fields.name: rows
fields.note: working rows: { s: digits, c: "rose"|"line", step }

fields.name: step
fields.note: reveal everything up to this step

views.description: A multiplication grid that sweeps to the answer cell (row × col).
A number line that hops from a start value — add/subtract or bond-up to a target.
A row of countable objects; counted-so-far light up one by one, with a running numeral. The counting/addition staple.

fields.name: highlight
fields.note: count up to this index; -1 for none

fields.name: numeral
fields.note: show a big total below; null to hide

views.description: A rectangle with its border traced to teach perimeter.
A number split into place-value columns (Ones | Tens | Hundreds…) plus its expanded form.
Repeated addition building toward a product (a + a + … = a × b).
A number line showing which nearby multiple a value rounds to.
A polygon with its sides counted and named.
Skip-counting hops along a track (count by 2s, 5s, 10s).
A word problem read aloud; words reveal as Luna reads, with numbers and action words colour-coded.

fields.name: revealed
fields.note: how many words shown so far

views.description: A shape/emoji folding along its line of symmetry.
A times table for n, revealing one fact at a time (skip-counting up the column).
A group of blocks with some removed to show subtraction as taking away.
A ten-frame filled with counters — the bridge to making tens.
A unit-conversion ladder (big ↔ small, e.g. m ↔ cm) showing the ×/÷ factor.
A box stacking unit cubes layer by layer to teach volume.
